circadianModal.title: Calorie Burn Science
circadianModal.subtitle: Circadian metabolic model · Live
circadianModal.scienceNote: Based on metabolic chamber and circadian biology research. Percentages are midpoints of published ranges for a typical adult with a standard sleep schedule and light daytime activity. Your actual burn varies with exercise, meal timing, and individual physiology.

phaseDesc.earlySlеep: Sleep onset and early sleep. Sympathetic withdrawal and falling core temperature drop your sleeping metabolic rate to roughly 85–95% of waking BMR.
phaseDesc.nadir: Biological low point. Core body temperature and resting energy expenditure hit their 24-hour minimum around 5 AM, aligned with the circadian nadir.
phaseDesc.morning: Sharp metabolic rise on waking. Simply being upright adds ~10% to your baseline. Morning TEF is at its daily peak — up to 2.5× stronger than an identical evening meal.
phaseDesc.midday: Metabolism is sustained near its daily high. Lunch triggers another TEF spike, and spontaneous movement (NEAT) from desk work or light tasks keeps output steady.
phaseDesc.peak: Circadian REE peak around 5 PM — roughly 10% above the early-morning nadir even at complete rest. Structured exercise in this window amplifies the burn significantly.
phaseDesc.evening: Dinner TEF is significantly blunted compared to morning. As your biological clock begins cooling core temperature and winding down sympathetic tone, metabolism slides back toward its nocturnal baseline.
